title: Parenting conflict with narrow settlement zone
scenario_summary: Two parents disagree sharply on overnight schedules for a child with school-related needs. Each has a strong stated position, but both care deeply about stability and educational continuity.
intended_challenge_type: Interest elicitation plus creative package/sequencing design.

public_goals: Reduce last-minute disruptions for the child.
interest_profile: Reduced conflict exposure for the child
starting_positions: Primary position: school-night overnights should remain limited and structured.
Secondary position: any expansion should be gradual and tied to demonstrated reliability.

public_goals: Obtain more overnight time with the child.
Avoid being treated as a secondary parent during the school week.
Create a schedule that feels fair and sustainable.
starting_positions: Primary position: more school-week overnights are appropriate now.
Secondary position: if routine is the concern, logistics should be solved rather than used to block time.
